desc:
From a small burrow below the trees the tunnel opens up into a wide cavern. The
ground is a mix of earth and black rock that shifts underfoot. Vile roots hang
from the ceiling, giving a disturbing caress to the heads of those with more
stature. To the east, the passage narrows into the darkness.
name: Tunnel Mouth

desc:
The earth and rock walls close to just a few feet in diameter. The dark, wet mud
looks like it could collapse further at the slightest disturbance. Small insects
crawl around, making it clear that not every creature is bothered by the dank
atmosphere.
name: Narrowing Tunnel

desc:
Thick roots jut out from every angle, but they have been crudely severed to form
a somewhat passable tunnel. Several of the cuts are fresh, while others were
clearly made some time ago. It is evident that there is a constant struggle to
keep the forest from reclaiming the earth below as its own.
name: Rough-hewn Tunnel

desc:
A steep, wide slope leads down from the forest into a dark, damp passageway. The
ground is smooth, indicating frequent use by travellers wishing to avoid the
wood above. The smooth trail leads west into a twisting knot of massive tree
roots.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The ceiling here is low, no more than six feet tall. Roots from the forest above
grow along the ceiling and walls, thinning out as the tunnel descends downward
into darkness. The floor of the tunnel slopes steeply downward. A thick layer of
mud and dense roots covers the northern wall.
name: Earthen Tunnel

desc:
The cavern is vast and expansive, with a smooth, level floor and a stony grey
wall. Large slabs of stone jut out randomly along the walls and the ceiling of
the cave is covered in a blanket of densely tangled roots from the forest above.
Off to the side, there is a steeply ascending tunnel that is lined with thick
tangles of roots that are inhabited by small insects.
name: Vast Underground Cavern

desc:
The cavern opens up into a huge space, with large rocks and boulders lining the
eastern and western walls. The tops of these rocks and boulders are covered in
thick blankets of green and black mosses. The air here is humid and heavy, and
some of the rocks to the west are wet with condensation. The ceiling of the
cavern rises up high and is covered in densely tangled roots. There is a large
crack in the floor that leads down into a deep, dark pit.
name: Wide Open Cavern

desc:
Large rocks and boulders line the walls of this vast, open cavern. The rocks
have thick blankets of black and green mosses that have settled and spread among
the tops of them. To the east, a large stack of flat stones lies neatly piled to
form steps that leads up to a narrow tunnel. The ceiling of the cavern is
covered in densely tangled roots from the forest above.
name: Vast Underground Cavern

desc:
Several feet of thick, black mud fill this pit in its entirety. Stones jut out
from the walls, forming a sort of natural staircase in which to ascend to the
cavern above. Insects crawl along the stone walls, disappearing into small
cracks here and there.
name: Deep Muddy Pit

desc:
Narrow and muddy, the floor of the tunnel drops off in the west, where a natural
stone staircase leads down into a vast cavern. The walls and ceiling are lined
with roots, and small insects crawl amongst them. The tunnel continues to the
east, where passage becomes more difficult.
name: Narrow Underground Tunnel

desc:
The air in this cavern is oppressively humid, and every surface is dangerously
slick. Every rock is covered in moss, although a few bare patches reveal that
the walls are made of granite. Roots protrude down through the cavern ceiling,
bursting through the rocks and climbing down the walls.
name: Humid, Mossy Cavern

desc:
Thick tangles of roots make passage in this narrow, underground tunnel tedious
and difficult. The floor of the tunnel is covered in thick, pungent mud with
roots mixed in. Thick roots cover the southern portion of the tunnel, where the
wall does not seem to be entirely solid.
name: Muddy Underground Tunnel

desc:
The midnight-black vines intertwine with the massive black oaks, creating a
dense mass of foliage. Each vine is covered in extremely sharp thornes, making
the canopy above a terrifying shroud. Fresh blood still drips from their cruel
spines, indicating that something has been entangled here recently. Their
seemingly intentional web looks as though any struggle after being caught would
only cause more damage and entangle the victim further.
name: Blood-dripping Spines

desc:
Formations of black, ragged rock surround this terrace on Dol Guldur. The
terrace lies high above the forest to the west and south and the way down
impossibly steep. Opposite these deadly dropoffs the hills rise to the east and
north. Only the north hill seems traversible, making this terrace a dead-end.
name: Terrace of Treacherous Rock

desc:
The area is completely void of any sort of trail, and the blackened foliage
pushes in on all directions. Commanding more attention than even the tree
branches or thick brush is the presence of an unnamed evil; it lingers in the
thickness. The black ground itself can very rarely be seen due to the dense
undergrowth, and visibility in all directions is limited at best.
name: Choked Underbrush

desc:
The rocky ground here is grooved with the traces of ancient currents, but the
water has long since dried up. The air remains humid with a strong odour of
decomposition. The soft parts of the tunnel wall have been shorn up with some
rough wooden pillars.
name: Bend in a Natural Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here is mostly of natural formation, probably hollowed out by an
underground current. No water flows here now, but a few damp spots on the floor
indicate there is still water above. The ground is mostly hard rock, but toward
the south it looks like it is changing into softer sand.
name: Natural Tunnel

desc:
The earth is soft and the air humid in this part of the tunnels, making it the
perfect habitat for the fungi that grow in abundance here. The tunnel is partly
natural but has clearly been widened to allow for an easier passage. The ceiling
is high enough for a man to walk upright without worry.
name: Fungus-covered Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel looks as though it was dug out with shovels. It is clearly not
entirely natural, as thick beams cross the ceiling supported by sturdy wooden
pillars. The earth floor is a rough mixture damp dirt and moist sand with a few
rocks and pebbles here and there. A heavy stone slab blocks the passage to the
west.
name: Excavated Tunnel

desc:
Several crude stone passages meet here. A large tunnel to the south looks to be
well used as the rock as been trodden to a smooth sheen. A short tunnel to the
east opens up into a room full of shelves. The walls look rough and natural as
if this was originally a cave.
name: Stone Intersection

desc:
Wooden shelves cover every wall of this room. They are packed with sacks of
wheat, salted meats, and other fare. In addition to the food, there are a number
of other curious objects, such as jars of unidentifiable liquid and a large
supply of iron chain. The floor is filled with barrels, except for a small
wooden door that leads below.
name: Storage Room

desc:
The cavern narrows here to a small hallway about 6 feet across. A few simple
wooden barriers have been set up, allowing for some defence should attackers
attempt to breach this part of the spire. A giant metal door covers most of the
south wall, evidently intended to hold even the strongest of creatures at bay.
The wall to the east is black stone, without any feature or adornment.
name: At a Guard Post

desc:
This large room serves a single purpose, that of keeping prisoners locked up.
There are no bars, only strong steel chains that are bolted into the solid stone
walls. The cell is totally dark, the floor covered by a smelly mass of dirt and
droppings. The stench lingering here leaves little doubt that those locked up
here recently were trolls or something equally filthy.
name: Deplorable Cell

desc:
There is a depression in the cavern's floor here, and the bottom of it is filled
with water. Unidentifiable brown and green things float in the water and the
pool gives off an unimaginable stench. The surrounding cavern's floor is
somewhat higher, a bit drier, and equally filthy.
name: Grimy Pool

desc:
The floor of this nasty cavern is covered in filth and droppings. A large amount
of it is piled against the eastern wall, possibly to keep the level of filth in
the rest of the cavern below waist level. Flies and roaches thrive in the filth.
name: Soiled Troll-pit

desc:
This cell is dark, bleak, and containing nothing but filth and grime. The walls
are of solid rock and looks like part of a hill or mountain, no amount of
pounding or digging is likely to get through them. The air is moist and full of
a lingering stench.
name: Filthy Cell

desc:
A huge underground pool of water fills the large cavern. The water is black,
still, and has a dark, ominous look about it. Smooth stone walls rise directly
from the water all around the pool, save a rocky, stench-ridden strip of land
which lies between the pool and the cavern walls on the eastern and southern
shores. The land forms a narrow, traitorous-looking path across the cavern. To
the north, the path transforms to a narrow corridor, quickly twisting into the
darkness. At the other end of the path, there is an old, rusty metal gate in the
stone wall, blocking the way to the west.
name: Underground Shore

desc:
This small, abandoned room has clearly been a prison cell in the past. Old,
rusty chains are hanging from the stone walls, and grey bones, skulls and
skeletons lie scattered around the room. A thick layer of dust and red stains is
vaguely visible on nearly all surfaces. The back wall of the dungeon is formed
of solid rock, whereas the other walls have been constructed from crude
boulders. The ceiling of this grim room is low, and the floor is made of sturdy,
square stone tiles.
name: Long-forgotten Dungeon

desc:
There is a broad gate to the south, consisting of two swinging doors, reinforced
with strips of charred metal. The trail goes on to the south and north of here.
A palisade has been built on both sides of the trail. To the north, higher
above, a fortified rampart provides a view over the trail.
name: Trail at an Iron-bound Gate

desc:
The trail from the gate runs into a sturdy wooden building here. To the east and
west a narrow path leads between this building and the camp walls. A large
iron-bound gate can be seen to the south. Above the trail here, a wooden
platform spans the length of the wall.
name: Before the Gates of the Camp

desc:
The trail in the camp here passes between the palisade to the south and a wooden
building to the north. The space here is quite limited, creating a corridor. But
there is still enough room to walk side by side or ride a horse. The ground is
quite hard, packed down from the tread of boot and hoof.
name: Trail in the Camp

desc:
A crude palisade wall rises from the ground here, its sharpened tips burnt and
blackened to fire-hardened points. Scaling this wall would be a dangerous
proposition at best. Rough weeds grow at the base of the wall, but the rest of
the ground is smooth earth packed down by the endless tread of heavy feet.
name: Rough Trail in the Camp

desc:
The trail takes a turn here, next to the guardtower to the west. The rugged
palisade lies to the south. Broken branches, roots and stumps litter the ground
at the base of the wall, remains of the impassable forest. The trail itself is
mostly clear, with just a few roots winding across like snakes.
name: At a Scarred Palisade

desc:
The trail in the camp makes a bend here. Westwards, the trail leads into a
narrow alleyway, while to the north it continues into the camp next to a wooden
building. There is a palisade wall to the south, and to the east, a wooden
guardtower is visible.
name: Bend in the Camp Trail

desc:
The camp trail comes to a crossroads here. To the north and south, the trail
continues, while to the east a short path leads to a wooden guardtower. A sturdy
wooden building stands to the west, much larger than the other structures.
name: Crossroads in a Trail

desc:
The camp trail comes to a fork here. To the west, a wide trail leads into some
sort of square or courtyard. The trail also continues to the north and south.
There is a wooden building to the east, with a large pile of basalt rocks
stacked up next to it. Black smoke rises from the chimney of the building.
name: Wide Trail

desc:
The camp trail continues south and north, with a bend to the southwest around a
wooden building. The trail has seen a lot of traffic here, leaving the ground
muddy and slippery. An opening to the west leads into a sturdy, but not
particularly well-constructed, wooden building. An odour of blood and meat
emanates from inside.
name: Trail next to a Warehouse

desc:
The trail is very muddy here with deep, thick boot prints leading up and down.
Some crude huts and tents have been set up to the east. A wooden building can be
seen to the west. A few discarded bones lying against the wall indicate that
this must be where food is served.
name: Muddy Trail

desc:
There is a four-way intersection of the trails here. The trail seems to continue
to the north and east, but it looks likely that the trail ends at the palisade
in both directions. To the northeast, a wooden guardtower is visible and to the
northwest, a large, well-built building stands tall.
name: Trail Junction

desc:
A few simple tents have been pitched here, evidently for some of the camp's less
important inhabitants. The only clear passage is back to the trail to the south,
as a crude but effective palisade has been built to the north, shielding the
camp from outside attacks. A guardtower rises above the palisade in the east,
and a well-built building is visible in the west.
name: Crude Palisade

desc:
The stable here is maintained surprisingly well, making it look out of the place
in the otherwise filthy camp. The walls are robust, made from good quality,
thick lumber. The earthen floor has been hardened by the weight of the animals.
A wooden trough provides water for the steeds, while a pile of hay serves as
food.
name: Sturdy Stable

desc:
Stacks of hay line the back wall of this stable, enough to feed several dozen
horses. A few barrels of water sit in a corner, while saddles and tack are hung
from the southern wall. The ground is a mix of muck and hay, somewhat squishy
underfoot. A few piles of manure indicate the stable is in serious need of a
good shovelling.
name: Back of the Stable

desc:
The path to the guard tower turns south here, as a wall of rough-cut tree trunks
form a wall to the east. The tower itself climbs high above the southern
palisade. The path under the tower is open, while a sturdy wooden building lies
directly to the north.
name: In Front of a Wooden Guard Tower

desc:
This corner of the camp is relatively quiet. A small dirt path leads off the
main trail toward the southeastern guard tower. To the west, a crude latrine has
been fashioned from rough wood planks and a few split logs. There is no door,
leaving anyone sitting on the latrine in full view of any unfortunate passerby.
name: Narrow Path

desc:
This muddy path winds its way between two buildings to the north and south.
There is a wooden door in the southern building, with a large iron handle. The
building itself is built from sturdy logs with a roof made of a combination of
leather, straws, branches all mixed with a mud sealant. The north building is
more of a shelter than a proper building, with open sides in several directions.
name: In Front of a Sturdy Building

desc:
A smell of burning carcasses emanates from the north as thick, black smoke rises
above the fence. It is made of sticks, branches, and thin logs attached together
with a rope. To the south, there is a sturdy building built of rough logs. Its
roof is a mixture of wood, leather, hay, and straw all sealed together with mud.
name: Narrow Alleyway

desc:
The trail becomes a narrow alley here between the two wooden buildings. The
sturdy building to the south is made of solid rough-cut logs, fastened together
with rope. The building to the north is also made of wood, but it appears
considerably less stout. Neither building has any windows.
name: Narrow Alley

desc:
The trail comes to a junction here, with paths continuing to the north, south,
and east. To the west, a small hut can be seen just off the trail. The ground is
smooth dirt, worn flat by the persistent beat of heavy boots. A few rocks and
weeds peek out amid the otherwise evenly stomped ground.
name: Crossing in the Camp Trail

desc:
There are some rocks on the trail here, making the path somewhat uneven. A
massive leather-roofed structure stands to the southwest, marking the corner of
the camp. A large rectangular building, made from thick hardwood, stands to the
east.
name: Trail in the Camp

desc:
The camp trail comes to an end here next to a wooden guardtower. A short path to
the north leads up to some stairs under the tower. To the south, a few small
huts and shacks are visible. A tall wooden palisade to the east separates the
camp from the wild.
name: Trail to a Guard Tower

desc:
The path through the camp is quite muddy and worn here. Most of the trees have
been cut down or ripped up, although there are a few smaller ones still living
beside to the path. The large building to the north dominates the area, while
some smaller buildings border the south side of the path.
name: Muddy Path in the Camp

desc:
A foul stench fills the air here. Something is burning, and it is not wood. A
muddy path with red, slimy footprints leads into a deep pit to the south. This
must be where the camp's food us prepared. A solid building lies to the north,
while the trail continues to the east and west.
name: Entrance to a Slaughter Pit

desc:
The camp trail passes through a small courtyard here. A gravel path, lined with
vicious metal spikes, leads through the courtyard to a large stone building. The
structure appears to be of higher quality than the rest of the camp. It is made
of uniform bricks of stone and it has a strong foundation made of larger stone
blocks.
name: Guarded Courtyard

desc:
This junction connects the east-west trail and north-south one. To the
northwest, a guardtower climbs above the corner of the camp. A large, solid
wooden building lies to the northeast. The trail is packed mud that gives a bit
with each step.
name: Trail Junction

desc:
To the west, a sturdy wooden building stands tall. The only entrance is a heavy
oaken door. Two crossed axes, painted in dark red blood, indicate this is
probably the barracks. The path to the door is well-trodden, apparently it is
used frequently. To the north, the path comes to a crossroads.
name: Before the Barracks

desc:
The trail through the camp narrows here. A large, wooden barracks stands to the
west, while a crude, make-shift shelter lies to the east. A few cut stumps
indicate this land used to be part of Mirkwood, but it has since been claimed
for some other purpose through a combination of harsh steel and hard toil.
name: Trail by the Barracks

desc:
The northeast guard tower rises above the camp here. Some crude stairs built
from roughly cut logs provide easy access to the platform above. The stairs are
covered in muddy footprints, indicating frequent use. The strong wooden palisade
protects the camp from the east and north.
name: Under a Guard Tower

desc:
This guardtower caps the northeastern corner of the camp. A gloomy forest is
clearly visible to the east while some desolated hills can be spotted to the
north. A thick leather canopy covers the guardtower from the sky above. A narrow
stairwell leads down to the camp below.
name: Guard Tower

desc:
Thick, grey smoke rises from a burning pile of animal carcasses, filling the air
with the acrid smell of burning flesh and fur. Bones lay forgotten around the
area, maybe left around as feed for the wargs of the camp. The path continues
northwards, where it widens into a more open area. And to the south, it looks
like there are more carcasses on the ground.
name: Pile of Burning Carcasses

desc:
There is a shallow pit dug in the ground. The very bottom of the pit is soaked
with blood, mixed with pieces of meat and shattered bone. A few axe-scarred
logs, stained red, form a circle on the pit's edge. Along the wall to the south,
a few carcasses, some animal, some humanoid, hang upside down. Below the
carcasses there are pots for collecting the dripping blood.
name: Slaughter Pit

desc:
A pungent stench hangs heavy inside this large wooden building. Rows of
partially butchered corpses hang from sharp meat hooks, while other cadavers are
thrown into large crates. Each crate has several holes in the bottom to allow
the blood to drain into a large pan set into the floor.
name: Butchery

desc:
This room has a few tables lined with simple wooden chairs. The tables are
covered with leftover food, pieces of bones and other inedible scraps. The stink
is heavy in this area, which has attracted a number of flies hungry for a meal.
name: Backroom

desc:
The trail comes to an abrupt stop at the wooden palisade. The side of the wall
is partly broken, evidently something crashed into it causing several of the
wooden logs to snap and splinter. A few boards have been nailed to what remains
to cover the gap. The ground in front of the palisade is mostly barren, save for
a few burnt stumps and chopped up roots.
name: Along a Palisade

desc:
A massive wooden guard tower looms above, on top of a buttress in the palisade.
Disfigured corpses dangle from some chains fixed to the walls, while birds and
insects are feasting on the remaining flesh. The forest has been cut and burned
back all around the tower and many charred stumps dot the ground.
name: Palisade under a Guard Tower

desc:
This enclosed yard is shielded off from the camp by a high wooden fence on three
sides, weapon racks line the fences, and the central area is kept clear for
practice. Blood stains the ground in several places, indicating that the weak
are shown no mercy here. A stairwell leads up into the tower above.
name: Fenced Duelling Yard

desc:
All the trees have been cut down here leaving numerous stumps poking out of the
ground. There is some twisted and thorny undergrowth left, and the land here
resembles more a barren field now than a forest. There is a crude but effective
palisade built to the west of here.
name: At a Crude Palisade

desc:
A narrow path here leads along the western palisade to a guard tower in the
southwest corner of the camp. Some wooden supports for the southern palisade
keep anyone from straying off the path to the east. A few wooden stumps lay
about, possibly used for relaxation after a long shift in the tower.
name: Path to the Guard Tower

desc:
A high guardtower has been built above the corner of the wooden palisade. A
narrow flight of stairs leads to the upper floor, standing on six strong poles.
Hides were used to shield the area between the poles from the wind and rain,
allowing sentries to take a quiet rest. Building tools and cruel weapons are
scattered around.
name: Ground Floor of a Guard Tower

desc:
This guard tower rises high above a cruelly spiked wooden palisade. It offers a
good view over the area in front of the massive southern gate, and the blackened
trail leading through it. A wide column of smoke dominates the southern view,
originating from a small hill seemingly ablaze. Westwards lies a dense and dark
forest. A small stair leads down through a hole to the ground floor.
name: In a High Guard Tower

desc:
Some branches of the forest reach out almost to the brink of the platform, but
they are not quite close enough to grab. The canopy of the forest is dense and
dark, blocking the view to the sky. To the north and below, a trail goes
straight through the camp and it bends to the east.
name: Wooden Platform

desc:
This part of the wooden platform offers a good view of both outside and inside
the camp. The wooden guardtowers in the corners of the camp are also clearly
visible from here. The road to Dol Guldur snakes away from the camp to the south
and into the forest to the west.
name: Wooden Platform

desc:
This spot on the platform offers a perfect view of the gate to the south, as
well as the road beyond. A fence has been built on the edges of the platform and
a few well-placed gaps provide ample sight lines against any would-be attackers.
name: Wooden Platform above the Gate

desc:
This platform stands at the top of the southern palisade. It is crafted from
rough wooden planks that creak with every step. To the west and south, the road
to Dol Guldur peeks out between the trees like a snake winding through a green
field.
name: Wooden Platform

desc:
This guard tower, built on top of a buttress in the palisade, offers a good view
of the gloomy forest just outside the wall. Several of the swaying trees in the
forest have grown as high as the tower. A crude rack for bows and arrows stands
in a corner on a particularly squeaky floor plank.
name: Inside a Wooden Guard Tower

desc:
This wooden guard tower rises in the corner of the palisade. It is covered by a
thatched roof of leather. Small gaps in the leather indicate that it is less
than waterproof. To the south, the forest is visible while to the east the bare
hills of Dol Guldur stand alone in the endless forest.
name: In a Wooden Guard Tower

desc:
A wooden palisade protects the southern and eastern edges of the camp here.
Clumsily built stairs lead up to the top of a guard tower in the corner of the
palisade. The tower is made of strong wooden timbers with a roof of thatched
leather.
name: Under a Wooden Guard Tower

desc:
The stench of rotten flesh and damp fur is heavy in this area. A large pit has
been dug into the ground, subdivided into numerous stalls. The walls are
reinforced with wooden planks and hardened earth, and they hold rusted shackles,
with sizes varying from that of a man to that of a troll. A steep ramp gives
access to the pit and stalls.
name: Warg Kennels

desc:
At the southern end of the stable, a mess of blood and wet goo covers the floor.
From the looks of the small footprints tracking through the muck, this must be
the whelping area for the wolves and wargs. There is plenty of food as as well.
A large trough is filled to the brim with scraps of meat and bone, mixed into a
rough paste with some flax and grass to hold it all together.
name: Pungent Stable

desc:
This wooden shed has been built against a stone building to the north, and the
heat radiating from a rough doorway suggests that it is the camp forge,
supplying this small armoury. Wide racks and tables line the walls, holding
cruel weapons and armour pieces of various types and sizes. The floor consists
of squeaky wooden planks, to prevent the heavy racks from sinking into the mud.
name: Armoury

desc:
The heat is intense in this small forge, sparks flying through the air and
coming to a sizzling rest on the floor, walls, and anyone walking around. Snagas
walk in every now and then with blocks of hardwood to fuel the kiln, while
others may operate the bellows, creating a scorching heat. A large anvil stands
in the centre of the room, surrounded by tongs, hammers, and half-finished
weapons and pieces of armour. A doorway to the south leads to the armoury.
name: Scorching Forge

desc:
This small patch of ground in the edge of the camp is drier than the
surroundings. A small canopy shields a pile of wood and branches from the
weather. Another overhang contains tools and materials for the production of
various potions and vials. The air is filthy from a huge column of black smoke
to the south. A muddy trail lies to the west.
name: Smoky Overhang

desc:
This sturdy stone guard house is built with care, as the main defensive
structure of the camp. Low walls and niches can be used by guards to ambush and
shoot at any invaders. A reinforced oaken door leads outside to the south. To
the east, a small archway leads into a much more well-appointed room.
name: Stone Guard House

desc:
A long table stands against the southern wall of this well organised study. It
is covered with charts, maps, and opened books. Flickering candles illuminate
the areas that the weak light from the high windows cannot reach. A winding
stairway along the eastern and northern wall leads up through a hole in the high
ceiling to a tower above. Numerous bookcases line the space under the stairs,
containing books on a wide variety of subjects, from dark magic to the history
of Mirkwood. This study clearly does not belong to an orc or troll.
name: Elaborate Study

desc:
There is a splendid view from the tall windows of this high tower. To the south,
the noisy, smoky camp bustles with activity. The eastern horizon is dominated by
the craggy hill and fortress of Dol Guldur, while to the west endless forests
stretch out. The room is apportioned with hand-carved furniture and thick, soft
rugs.
name: Private Chamber in a Tower

desc:
This crude structure is not well-built. The wooden walls are so light and so
poorly held down that it appears a stiff breeze might topple them. Some tattered
fabric and a bed of hay indicates that someone probably sleeps here. A second
room lies to the north.
name: Shabby Building

desc:
This room is very dark. A thick roof made of a combination of sticks, branches,
planks, leather, and mud ensures no light enters from above. The dirt floor is
somewhat soft and moist, indicating the roof is probably not watertight. A hole
in the floor leads into the earth below, but any stairs there may have been have
long since rotted away.
name: Backroom of a Shabby Building

desc:
Tables and benches are loosely scattered around this large room. It provides a
suitable place for the orkish guards to drink their brains into oblivion, and
when needed run to battle, if they can still find their own weapon. A thick
wooden door in the eastern wall leads to the camp, while a rough arched opening
to the south gives access to a wide yard.
name: Inside the Orc Barracks

desc:
Rows of crude slabs covered in furs line the walls of this massive hall. Near
the back, small closable compartments are available for more demanding orcs,
while lowly snagas can just rest on the bare, filthy floor. There are no
windows, but the chaotic noise of the camp still penetrates the thin walls.
name: Orc Sleeping Chambers

desc:
Despite the noisy activity outside, this circular, windowless hut is very quiet.
A round table is placed around the central supporting pole, covered with jars
and pots containing unknown body parts. Strange-looking necklaces and medallions
hang from a small altar of worship at the northern end, and every surface is
dotted with smoky candles, their fumes escaping through a hole in the tip of the
cone-shaped ceiling.
name: Silent Hut

desc:
This appears to be one end of a collapsing passage. The tunnel continues to the
south. The roof appears to be on the verge of collapsing further. Some water
drips slowly down from the decaying roof, forming small puddles that dot the
ground like freckles.
name: In a Ruined Passage

desc:
Time and shoddy construction have brought this passage to the brink of collapse.
Several ceiling planks have already broken under the strain of the earth above.
Clots of fresh mud litter the floor in small piles. The poles holding back the
wall have started to lean inwards as the walls close in. The tunnel seems
sturdier to the south.
name: Collapsing Passage

desc:
A narrow passage winds its way through the earth here. While to the north
several cave-ins indicate instability in the tunnel, this section looks solid
and well-maintained. Thick, wooden support pillars look as if they have been
installed recently.
name: In a Sturdy Passage

desc:
This cellar might have been in use at some point, but it is now abandoned. The
floor is a mess of upended tables with a thick coating of dust on every surface.
A wooden stair has been built against the stone wall, leading up to a hole in
the ceiling. A narrow passage leads north.
name: Abandoned Stone Cellar

desc:
Several benches set around this room allow the prison guards respite while they
wait to thwart any escape or rescue attempts. A thick metal door with an
iron-barred window is set into the southern wall. The tunnel continues to the
west and east.
name: Prison Guard Room

desc:
This narrow passage was dug in a hurry, judging by the shoddy workmanship. The
low ceiling consists of wooden planks, bulging under the weight of the earth
above, while mouldy and rotten wall panels barely keep the tunnel from caving
in.
name: Shoddy Passage

desc:
Rusted chains are still attached to the walls, but this underground prison
cannot be used to lock up anyone. The metal-barred door that would normally
block the doorway to the north has been ripped off its hinges. It now lies
useless on the floor amidst the rubble and loose earth. Large roots stick out of
the ceiling and walls, some dangling menacingly in the dusty air.
name: Ruined Prison

desc:
This end of the guardhouse is dominated by a dark hole in the middle of the
floor. Some old looking stairs lead down into the hole, though they seem barely
able to hold together. A musty smell emanates from below, evidently there is not
much ventilation down there.
name: Rickety Stairs

desc:
This room seems to be well-used judging from the filth and garbage laying about.
In the centre of the room, a rough table made of a couple of wooden planks
surrounded by some stumps of wood serves as the only furniture. On the centre of
the table, the remains of a pig are left forgotten. It seems to attract a lot of
flies.
name: Guardhouse

desc:
This is a typical, if a bit dirty, guardhouse. Against the southern wall a
couple of weapon racks provide easy access to arms should the need arise. The
building has more rooms to the west but the wooden walls and lack of windows
make them difficult to see.
name: Entrance to a Guardhouse

desc:
Crude wooden beams hold up the walls of this underground storage room. The walls
are lined with dozens of weapon racks and armour stands. Many of the racks are
locked with chains. The equipment appears of good quality, but judging by the
dust that has settled on the floor, this room is not visited often.
name: Musty Storage

desc:
This is a small room, more like a closet. The walls are bare wood, and the floor
is made of sturdy planks. There are some spider webs hanging in the roof and
some dust and dirt has accumulated on the floor. The air is a bit musty. A few
wrinkled papers and some pieces of broken pens lie about.
name: Hidden Cache

desc:
The rock has been hollowed here to form a small bedroom. A wooden desk is set
near the eastern wall. It is covered with illegible notes and crude anatomical
sketches of what appear to be trolls. The bed is human-sized, with coarse woolen
coverings.
name: Small Bedroom

desc:
This sanctum has a staircase running through it, leading both up and down. Dark
worship and dreadful experiments take place here, judging from the table covered
with scribbled notes in the dark tongue of Mordor, and old books bound in
strange leathers. There are phials and bottles full of foul fluids and others
full of strange, swirling mists. Another desk has chains at the ends and a deep
channel to collect blood. An object much like an altar stands along one wall,
surrounded by a veil of darkness that never seems to dissipate or disperse.
name: Dark and Eerie Sanctum

desc:
A wide stairwell rises through a cone of black rock. The wide steps are simply
crafted but functional. Every dozen or so steps, there is a platform where the
stairs turn and leads further up and down. Dark shadows play on the walls, as if
cast by strange shapes that are not really here.
name: Wide, Shadowy Stairwell

desc:
Wide stone steps rise in a spiral on the inside of a cone of black rock. A solid
hatch has been placed at an opening at the very top of the stairs, blocking
access to whatever is hidden above. The walls are pitted and rough. They appear
natural as opposed to the steps which were clearly carved to fashion a
staircase.
name: Foreboding Stairwell

desc:
A rotting stench hangs in this cramped prison cell. Chains are attached to the
walls and some are still locked to long deceased prisoners. Some have started to
decay, becoming food for a variety of crawly creatures. Others are bare bone,
having already served as nourishment. The skeletons show signs of torture. Many
have broken bones or missing fingers, one is even missing the lower half of its
jaw. A sturdy barred door is set into the northern wall.
name: Stinking Prison

desc:
A narrow crevice cuts a path through black and dark grey rocks. The walls press
in on the narrow passageway on both sides as the crevice tunnels through the
rock towards the north. Jagged rocks provide plenty of steps leading up towards
an opening in the rock.
name: Narrow Crack

desc:
The cave cuts through jagged black and dark grey rocks. The walls press in on
the narrow passageway leaving very little room to manoeuvre. The floor tilts at
an unsettling angle, the evidence of the mountain's distant past, eons ago when
the earth shifted and tore open this gash in the rock.
name: Narrow Crack in the Rocks

desc:
Broken chunks of dark, almost black rock line the walls in this area. On the
edges of the jagged chunks, a lighter grey rock can be seen. A faint draught of
air can be felt coming from the north and the east, indicating that there might
be more to this cavern behind the fallen rocks. A deep breach in the rock below
is where most of the fallen rocks have ended up.
name: Cavern in the Jagged Rocks

desc:
This area of the cave is comprised of vertical blocks of dark stone of various
heights. At first glance it looks like someone must have carved them, but upon
closer inspection it seems natural. Some of the columns along the wall reach
from floor to ceiling, but in the middle of the cave the broken columns hang
from above.
name: Cavern of Vertical Columns

desc:
Naturally formed vertical columns of coal-black stone have broken off the wall
in such a way that it looks like it would be possible to climb up them to a
higher part of the cavern. The floor of the cave is littered with the fallen
chunks of the stone columns that have broken and crumbled. Stacks of crumbled
rocks are also resting against the walls.
name: Staircase of Pillars

desc:
The naturally formed pillars of dark rock seem to form steps leading down into
the darkness below. It looks like it would be possible to climb down in that
direction. The sound of dripping water can be heard echoing off the walls of the
cavern from down below. The jagged tunnel continues off to the east from here as
well.
name: Rock Shelf above Some Pillars

desc:
The roof of the cave slants off to the north, and the floor slants off to the
south here making it rather difficult to walk through this area. The walls of
the cave crumble when touched, and rocks and dust have accumulated on the
ground. The faint sound of dripping can be heard from the west, and the cave
continues off into the darkness to the east.
name: Slanted Cave

desc:
Large chunks of dark grey basalt stone are spaced in such a way that it looks
like it might be possible to climb down into the darkness below. The cavern
continues off to the west at a tilted angle. On the northern wall of the cave,
there is a long crack in the stone that is about twenty feet long. Probably the
remnants of a shift in the stone from thousands of years ago.
name: Stone above a Winding Passage

desc:
Stalactites hang from the ceiling like thousands of stone spikes, and the
constant dripping has formed crystal clear pools of water on the ground. The
floor is also covered with stalagmites reaching upwards towards the roof of the
cave causing the footing to be quite treacherous. Some of the stone has managed
to connect from floor to ceiling, creating glistening columns.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
It isn't possible to see very far ahead in the cavern due to the twists and
turns within the passage. The massive mountain above presses down heavily on
this winding gap, threatening to crush anyone or anything foolish enough to
enter the narrow space. It looks like the cave opens up a little more to the
north.
name: Winding Passage

desc:
This area of the cave opens up a bit and branches off in three directions. To
the south a narrow passageway can be seen winding off into the darkness, and
that same fissure in the rock continues off to the east. It also looks like it
might be possible to clamber up the stones here to a higher level of the cave
above as well.
name: Branch in a Winding Passage

desc:
The narrow fissure in the rock has apparently come to a dead end here. The gap
narrows down so small to the east that only the smallest creature could
continue. Whatever earth-shattering force created this rift in the stone so many
millennia ago didn't have the strength to tear any further in that direction.
name: Eastern End of a Winding Passage

desc:
A barely perceptible flow of crystal clear water streams slowly from pool to
pool in a southerly direction. The water appears to flush down into the cracks
in the rock where it is no longer possible to follow. The walls, ceiling, and
floor all glisten and sparkle with moisture. The stalactites and stalagmites
occur in a variety of colours from dark grey to brown, and some are even a
nearly white colour.
name: Glistening Cave

desc:
A natural tunnel extends north-south, creating an easily travelled passageway. A
small hole leads down into the darkness, and muffled sounds can be heard from
that direction. In spite of the small size of the opening, the worn edges and
scratch marks indicate frequent travel to and from below.
name: Connecting Passage

desc:
Broken crates and barrels, torn and dented armour, broken weapons, and other
rubbish lie in haphazard heaps on the floor of this cave. There is enough
equipment and supplies here to outfit a small army if any of it were fit for
use, a testament to the many souls who have braved these labyrinths, never to
return.
name: Rubbish Pile

desc:
The tunnel passage winds into the distance. Sharp, black glass-like crystals
protrude from the walls and ceiling like daggers. Ragged articles of clothing,
stained with dried blood, hang from a few of the crystals, apparently torn from
previous travellers as they passed through this treacherous place.
name: Jagged Tunnel

desc:
Coarse, black basalt in the northern part of this tunnel changes abruptly to
glistening crystal formations to the east. The crystals are sharp, and passage
to the east could become perilous. The tunnel seems to expand into a much larger
cave to the north.
name: Transition

desc:
Large basalt columns extend up from the floor at random intervals, disappearing
into the darkness above. Crudely drawn pictographs depicting what appear to be
scenes of war are scrawled onto the stone walls with some sort of indelible
white paint.
name: Black Cavern

desc:
A large cavern opens into the darkness, the walls extend upward out of sight.
Every noise echoes repeatedly off the smooth stone, mixing with other sounds to
create dissonant chords that ebb and flow as new sounds enter the mix while
others fade out.
name: Dark Echoing Chamber

desc:
The walls and ceiling of this tunnel are crumbling, littering the floor with
rubble. There is evidence that portions of this tunnel have clearly been worked,
but the surrounding earth was apparently too unstable to allow mining to
continue safely.
name: Ruined Tunnel

desc:
Jagged marks on the walls and ceiling show that the original tunnel has been
widened in several places. Debris and broken mining equipment lie discarded in
neat piles near the areas that have been expanded. Clearly this was an active
mining operation at some point, but it appears to have been prematurely halted.
name: Mined Tunnel

desc:
With several abrupt changes in direction, this twisting tunnel is quite
disorienting, and the rough and uneven floor, covered with loose rubble, makes
it difficult not to stumble while navigating through the tortuous path this
tunnel becomes.
name: Twisting Tunnel

desc:
A vaulting tunnel ceiling opens up to reveal giant holes scattered across the
surface, as if worms had eaten away at the stone. The many passageways continue
into darkness as if unending. The floor is covered in filth and debris, and
sticky grime is plastered onto the tunnel walls.
name: Intersecting Tunnel

desc:
The walls of the tunnel are covered with shallow pin-sized holes scattered all
over the surface. Cobwebs drape the tunnel ceiling, appearing as large as
curtains in certain sections, hanging down to the floor. The layers of web
curtains obscure and distort the view in every direction, creating a surreal
atmosphere.
name: Pitted Tunnel

desc:
This alcove seems to be some sort of antechamber. There are elaborate patterns
of intertwined trees carved in the walls and ceiling. The patterns are inset
with a lustrous metal giving the impression of being in a moonlit forest. To the
east, the glistening images of two large oaks with intertwined branches form an
archway.
name: Entrance

desc:
Two tunnels come together in a perfect corner, neat and square, clearly the work
of highly skilled craftsmen. The walls show remnants of intricate patterns and
writing that appears to be Elven, but time and erosion has faded both into
obscurity.
name: Sharp Turn

desc:
Faded tool marks, edges worn smooth with time imply that this tunnel was made
ages ago. The air is completely motionless, but somehow neither stale nor fresh.
This place gives an aura of unrest as if some arcane energy is somehow trapped
and struggling to escape.
name: Disquieting Delve

desc:
There is a sense that this tunnel continues for an eternity in both the north
and south directions. The utter darkness is sobering, creating a feeling of
unease in even the most hardy. No exits can be seen, but there is a sense of
expansiveness to the south and an unsettling aura to the north.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A tunnel from the south meets an east-west tunnel at this junction. The floor is
worn from heavy and frequent foot traffic east and west, but the south passage
is covered with dust and shows no evidence of use. Whatever lies to the south,
it does not seem to interest those who travel here.
name: Junction

desc:
The floor is covered with sticky, viscous slime, which seems to originate from
somewhere to the south. The putrid smell indicates that it is some kind of
animal waste. Faint scratching sounds can be heard all around, as if hundreds of
tiny creatures are scurrying about searching for something.
name: Feculent Alcove

desc:
Cocoons of every shape and size hang from the ceiling on thick strands of
thread. Movement inside many of the cocoons indicates that the occupants must
still be alive. The floor is littered with empty and broken cocoons, bones from
every sort of creature, and lifeless, empty corpses, shrivelled and dried as if
every ounce of liquid, and life, had been sucked from them.
name: Food Storage

desc:
This narrow tube in the stone extends sharply up and down. Sticky, silky strands
span across the opening, making it difficult to move. Several bones and a few
full skeletons are trapped in the web, their original owners apparently having
fallen from above to become trapped here and be picked clean by the local
inhabitants.
name: Steep Hole

desc:
A steep tunnel coming down from the east transitions to a gentle slope leading
to the west. A slight draught comes from the east, bringing with it a stale,
musty odour. Every now and again, the sound of small rocks falling can be heard
echoing from the west.
name: Bottom of a Steep Tunnel

desc:
A crude tunnel continues into the darkness. The walls are rough and marked with
haphazardly arranged jagged cuts that continue down the entire length of the
wall. Small veins of black glittering minerals run along the edges. The floor is
covered with layers of dust, concealing shards of crushed, translucent black
glass.
name: Mined Tunnel

desc:
The floor of this tunnel slopes very steeply downward from east to west, nearly
as steep as a stairway, but without the benefit of steps to help with footing. A
tiny stream of water splashes swiftly down a narrow gutter, disappearing into
some sort of drain to the west.
name: Steeply-sloped Tunnel

desc:
Two narrow tunnels, one from the west and one from the north meet at a perfect
right angle here. The featureless walls rise to meet an equally featureless
ceiling above. A trickle of water, formed by condensation of the wet, humid air
on the cold stone walls, runs from the north and down to the west, flowing in a
small channel that appears to have been intentionally carved to collect moisture
and prevent it from accumulating on the smooth floor.
name: Narrow Corner

desc:
Perfectly square and evenly-spaced stairs have been carved in the stone, leading
down to the south and extending upwards to the north. The smooth black walls
glisten with condensation, and small droplets fall from time to time from the
ceiling above.
name: Carved Stone Stairs

desc:
The tunnel makes a sharp turn here, rising up to the east and sloping down to
the south. The walls and floor have been skilfully crafted from the stone,
smooth and even, but not slick, despite the dampness from the humid air. A
draught can be felt rising from the south.
name: Curved Tunnel

desc:
A passageway leads downwards to the west from a smoothly-carved landing that
extends to the east. The clean, square lines show that this tunnel was created
by master craftsmen, though there are no decorative or other distinguishing
marks of any kind. To the west, the downward slope is smooth and gentle, making
movement through this tunnel is easy.
name: Smooth Sloping Passageway

desc:
This tunnel is wet, filled with mud, slime and pooled water. The air feels more
drinkable than breathable, and every step splashes water. A dark hatch carved
from black stone can be seen set into the muddy ceiling. To the west the tunnel
continues into the darkness.
name: Dank Tunnel

desc:
A moderate sized tunnel made primarily of dark earth ends abruptly here. It must
have been well used at some point, as the ground in the middle has been packed
down into a solid earthen path. However, a landslide of thick, brown earth and
small rocks has made any passage farther entirely impossible. A hatch, carved
from black stone, lies in the centre of the passage.
name: Eroded Tunnel

desc:
This square room looks like a natural cavern expanded on to make room for the
numerous crates, shelves, and cabinets that occupy it. The room, being
underground, is chill even during summer. An odour of rancid meat fills the air.
A narrow stair leads up from this larder, it is much too narrow for the cabinets
here; the tunnel disappearing into darkness to the west seems a more likely
origin.
name: Rancid Larder

desc:
A tunnel from the west ends abruptly here. A pile of boulders, rocks of all
sizes, and other debris blocks the passage in all other directions. What remains
of the low, uneven ceiling appears unstable and likely to collapse at any
moment.
name: Partially Collapsed Tunnel

desc:
A large bastion has been built into the palisade wall to guard the western side
of the camp against attack, and this broad guard tower stands on top. Thin holes
in the walls allow one to watch the strip of cleared land outside the palisade
while staying hidden from view. Racks for bows and arrows stand along the
eastern wall, near a stair leading down. Past attacks on the tower have left a
section of the western wall heavily scarred and dented.
name: In a Broad Guard Tower
